description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a distinctive pattern of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The stone appears to be a composite material, possibly granite or terrazzo, with a speckled mix of light pink, black, and gray tones. The grooves are deep and create a strong visual contrast with the solid black material visible in the gaps and along the sides. The block is thick and robust, with clean, sharp edges and a flat upper surface, making it suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments. The sides are solid black, suggesting either a different material or a coating for durability. There are no visible signs of weathering, engravings, or additional features beyond the grooved pattern.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on top and a speckled pink-black surface, bordered by solid black sides. The grooves create a striking striped pattern, making it a visually distinct urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface and the solid black face beneath the grooves.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the black side panel prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved pattern and solid black base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the solid black side.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a decorative barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering or erosion; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced in a row as a barrier
